english: Tibet; ṕyogs-ris (phyogs ris) party; Cs. has also: raṅ-ris (rang ris) one's own party, gźaṅ-ris (gzhan ris) another's party, ris-ċan (ris can) partial, prejudiced, ris-méd (ris med) impartial, indifferent, hence also hermit, because he ought to feel indifferent to every thing. — 3. Sch.: 'ris-su (ris su) difference, ris-su čad-pa (ris su chad pa) equality'(??). — 4. ris-gza (ris gza') symb. num.: 7, derived from the number of the great planets together with sun and moon.

1. horn, = rwa (rwa); rá-ru (ra ru) goat's horn, lúg-ru (lug ru) ram's horn. — 2. parts of vessels etc. resembling a horn, e.g. the handle of a stew-pan Mil. nt.; *gó-ru* (sgo ru) C. door-post. — 3. part, division, dmág-gi (dmag gi) of an army Stg., wing Cs.; of a country, dbu-gtsáṅ-ru-bźi (dbu gtsang ru bzhi) Mil.; gyás-ru (g.yas ru) the right side or wing, gyóṅ-ru (g.yon ru) the left side or wing, gźuṅ-ru (gzhung ru) the middle part or centre Cs. — 4. as num. figure: 85. Comp. ru-dár (ru dar) Wdk, Mil., ru-mtsón (ru mtshon) Sch. military ensign, banner, colours, ṕyár-ba (phyar ba) to display, to hoist (a flag). — ru-sná (ru sna) division of an army Sch. — rú-pa (ru pa) 'troops, advanced posts of the enemy' Sch. — ru-dpón (ru dpon) commander of a regiment, colonel.
hatred, grudge, malice, (of rare occurrence); ru-ṅa-ċan (ru nga can) spiteful, malicious.
Cs.: 'a kind of spicy root'; in Lh. Inula Helenium.
Cs. n. of a district in Tibet contiguous to Ladak; an extensive plain, east of lake Paṅkoṅ.
a tent-covering made of yak's hair; rú-ba-pa (ru ba pa) a person living in such a tent; rú-bai tsogs (ru ba'i tshogs) a number of such tents, a tent-village.
curdled milk, used as a ferment C., ,ó-mar rú-ma blug-,dra ('o mar ru ma blug 'dra) as when sweet and curdled milk are put together S.g.; as to its effect, it may also stand for leaven.
Med.; Cs.: a sort of berry
Stg.; Sch.: a kind of deer; a species of fruit-trees.
'flat-horn', acc. to Sch. the reindeer (śá-ba ru-léb (sha ba ru leb) the domesticated, and ,bróg-gi ru-léb ('brog gi ru leb) the wild r.), more prob. the elk, v. ḱa-śa (kha sha).
appearing (?), źiṅ snum rug-gé (zhing snum rug ge) the field had a luxuriant appearance Mil. nt.
1. Cs. a kind of potato. 2. W. to collect, gather, pluck, v. sgrúg-pa (sgrug pa).

1. vb. to be fit, calculated, suitable, right, and adj.: fit etc., gen. with termin., rarely with the root of the verb, tsig ,di ,jigs-su ruṅ (tshig 'di 'jigs su rung) this word is calculated to terrify, is terrible Dzl.; btsoṅ-du ruṅ (btsong du rung) it is salable, vendible Dzl.; slob-dpón-du mi ruṅ (slob dpon du mi rung) he is not fit to be a teacher Dzl.; gźán-du mi ruṅ (gzhan du mi rung) he is good for nothing else, but also in the sense: he is too good for anything else, nothing inferior can be offered to him Glr.; grub rúṅ-du yód-pa (grub rung du yod pa) one that is able to perform it Tar.; mi rnyed mi ruṅ (mi rnyed mi rung) it must be procured by all means Dzl., mi byar mi ruṅ (mi byar mi rung) it must be done Dzl.; nyál-du mi ruṅ (nyal du mi rung) it would not do to sleep Dzl.; med kyaṅ ruṅ (med kyang rung) I (you etc.) can also do without (him) Glr.; dei tse gtáṅ-du rúṅ-ṅam mi ruṅ (de'i tshe gtang du rung ngam mi rung) would it not be as well to let him go once more? Dzl.; ċi-ltar yid-čes-su ruṅ (ci ltar yid ches su rung) how can one believe you? Dzl.; *kon-ċóg zun zer mi ruṅ* (dkon mchog rdzun zer mi rung) W. God cannot tell a lie; ,di yaṅ ruṅ tsam ('di yang rung tsam) it may perhaps be used instead Wdn.; ṅá-la mós-pa ma byas kyaṅ rüṅ-ste (nga la mos pa ma byas kyang rung ste) that they do not show me any honour is not so great a loss; but... Mil.; ,dis ruṅ-ṅam ('dis rung ngam) is that the right thing? will that do? de-ltar ruṅ (de ltar rung) (W. *čog* (chog)) well, let it be so! for aught I care! — 2. several other phrases with ruṅ: a. lus ,di ċi ruṅ (lus 'di ci rung) why should we care so much for this our body? Dzl.; esp. ċi ma ruṅ (ci ma rung), preceded by na (na) or (rarely) by yaṅ (yang): why should
